title: Cheilanthes lucida
intro: Cheilanthes lucida, also known as the shining lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Woodsiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Cheilanthes lucida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Cheilanthes lucida is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically, it belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which consists of ferns. Within this division, it falls under the class Polypodiopsida. The order Polypodiales is where Cheilanthes lucida is classified, and it is further grouped into the family Pteridaceae. This family is commonly known as the wood fern family. At the species level, Cheilanthes lucida is the accepted name for this plant.
